subtasks.description: Query SQL warehouse to retrieve Datadog license allocations (by license tier/type), actual usage metrics (events, dashboards, monitors, log volume), and historical time-series data to establish growth patterns over the past 12 months
Query MongoDB to retrieve current Datadog subscription state, entitlements, configured thresholds, and any usage warnings or overage records

Reconcile SQL usage metrics and growth trends against MongoDB subscription state and entitlements. Classify current posture (under-utilized, optimized, at-risk, overagebound). Identify utilization gaps, predict near-term capacity needs, and generate optimization recommendations based on growth trajectory

plan.cost_rationale: Three subtasks: (1) sql_gen at mid tier for multi-metric usage query with time-series grouping and growth calculation; (2) mongo_query at small tier for straightforward subscription state retrieval; (3) cross_recon at frontier tier because reconciliation requires synthesizing disparate data sources (allocations vs. actuals), inferring growth patterns, classifying multi-dimensional posture (utilization, cost risk, capacity), and generating tailored recommendations—this demands high-level reasoning across signals. The plan minimizes subtasks by combining all SQL metrics into one query and all MongoDB state into one query, avoiding artificial splitting.

output.sql: SELECT cf.license_id, cf.publisher, cf.product, cf.fiscal_year, cf.fiscal_quarter, DATE_TRUNC('month', cf.event_date)::DATE AS month, cf.event_type, COUNT(DISTINCT cf.event_id) AS event_count, SUM(cf.daily_active_seats) AS total_active_seats, SUM(cf.amount_usd) AS usage_amount_usd, COUNT(DISTINCT cf.user_id) AS unique_users FROM consumption_facts cf WHERE cf.publisher = 'Datadog' AND cf.event_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '12 months' GROUP BY cf.license_id, cf.publisher, cf.product, cf.fiscal_year, cf.fiscal_quarter, DATE_TRUNC('month', cf.event_date)::DATE, cf.event_type ORDER BY cf.license_id, month DESC
